The motion is denied as to requests for admission Nos. 1-12 and granted as to Nos. 13-21. Plaintiffs are sanctioned $60 (filing fee). Please attach a copy of the judge pro tem's report to the order. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: TAMMY TRAVERSO VS. PACIFIC MARITIME ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant Pacific Maritime Association
rulings.Rulings: October 9, 2019 Line 3, PLAINTIFF TAMMY TRAVERSO'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant Pacific Maritime Association Pro Tem Judge William Lynn, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. The court notes that both parties failed to properly comply with CRC 3.1345. As presented, the court cannot properly rule on the motion with the separate statements provided. The court also notes that the motion improperly combines 2-4 motions into one. The court is additionally concerned that it may not have jurisdiction to hear the motion as the motion to compel further responses to both sets of discovery requests appears to fall outside of the statutory 45-day period, and none of the declarations show an agreement to extend the statutory deadline. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to williamclynn@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (jpt/302).

If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (jpt/302).

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOE VS. BIJOU-CENTURY, LLC (DBA NEW CENTURY THEATRE) ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wed, October 9, 2019, Line 7, DEFENDANT CITIGROUP INC., CITIBANK, N.A.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action. Defendant Citibank, N.A.'s motion to compel arbitration is denied without prejudice. The motion raises an unresolved threshold issue: whether plaintiff may sue using a fictitious name. This is permitted in "extraordinary circumstances," but a "prior court order is required." (Edmon & Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) §6.90.10; see also Starbucks Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1436, 1452 n.7 ("judicial use of Doe plaintiffs"; "order which maintains the confidentiality of John Doe's identity" (emphasis added; quote marks deleted).) The register of actions indicates that this issue has been raised in the case, but not resolved by court order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats the tentative verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. JOSEPH PATRICK GILLACH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joseph Patrick Gillach'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: October 9, 2019 Line 2 2), DEFENDANTS JOSEPH GILLACH, PROGRESSIVE PROPERTY GROUP INC., FRANK BOWERS, CAR TOWN SANTA CLARA LLC'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge William Lynn, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. Defendants should be prepared to address the scope of the motion in light of Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 Cal. App. 4th 390, 408-409. Plaintiff should be prepared to address the issue of whether its tardy discovery responses met the requirements of the Civil Discovery Act in light of Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 Cal. App. 4th 390, 408-409. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to williamclynn@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (jpt/302).

Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 Cal. App. 4th 390, 408-409. Plaintiff should be prepared to address the issue of whether its tardy discovery responses met the requirements of the Civil Discovery Act in light of Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 Cal. App. 4th 390, 408-409. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to williamclynn@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (jpt/302).

rulings.Case Title: ISMAIL DOONGERWALA M.D ET AL VS. MAPLEBEAR, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wed, October 9, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT MAPLEBEAR, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant Maplebear Inc.'s motion to strike punitive damages is denied. The complaint pleads that plaintiff was struck by a motor vehicle operated for defendant's grocery delivery service which allegedly puts a premium on speed. The driver was allegedly a 25-year-old who had been on disability for seven years with Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder, yet defendant "made no effort to determine anything about" his "background." This more than suffices at the pleading stage to allege a corporate defendant's conscious disregard for the safety of others. (Civ. Code §3294.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats the tentative verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
